implementation_scope: source alignment and canonical problem normalization
syllabus-aware problem-type classification and tag evidence
classified catalog UI with level, algorithm-domain, problem-type, and knowledge-point pages

decisions.decision: Separate official syllabus facts from inferred/community labels.
For GESP level 5 + dynamic programming, show an out-of-level or boundary warning because DP is not level 5; simple DP is level 6 and complex DP is level 7.
Every problem-type tag and knowledge-point tag must have provenance, evidence, confidence, and review status.
Decide whether full problem statements can be stored after license and source-term review.

recommendations.text: Start with official C++ level 5 PDFs and the official syllabus before adding broad crawling.
Build classified catalog pages first: level page, algorithm-domain page, problem-type page, problem detail, and source alignment page.
Use rule-based labels plus manual review before introducing LLM or embedding classification.

open_questions: Will the MVP support only C++ level 5 or also Python level 5?
Do you want to store full statements, or only metadata plus links until authorization is clear?
Is the first audience students, parents, or teachers?
